tasks.name: Save the 3 highest‑funded African freshwater biodiversity projects (2018–2022) to a comparison list
tasks.description: Find freshwater biodiversity projects in Africa that started between 2018 and 2022, filter to show only projects with at least $1,000,000 funding, sort them by highest funding, and save the top 3 projects into a new list called "Africa Freshwater 2018-22".
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the "Projects" option in the main navigation menu.
Click the keyword search box on the Projects page and type "freshwater biodiversity", then press Enter to run the search.
In the sidebar filters, click the "Region" dropdown and select "Africa".
Click the "Status" filter and select the "Ongoing" checkbox.
Find the "Start Year" range control and set the minimum year to 2018 and the maximum year to 2022.
In the "Funding Amount" filter, type "1000000" into the minimum funding field and leave the maximum field empty.
Open the sort dropdown above the results and choose "Funding – High to Low".
On the results list, click the bookmark or "Save" icon on the first project card.

In the save dialog, select "Create new list", type "Africa Freshwater 2018-22" as the list name and confirm, then close the dialog and click the bookmark or "Save" icon on the second and third project cards, selecting the existing "Africa Freshwater 2018-22" list each time.

tasks.name: Subscribe to updates for a 2024 coastal restoration project in South America
tasks.description: Locate a coastal restoration project in South America with fieldwork scheduled in 2024 and subscribe to project updates using the on‑site subscription form with the email address "coastal.observer@example.org".
steps: From the homepage, click the "Projects" item in the main navigation.
On the Projects page, open the "Topic" filter and select "Coastal restoration".
Open the "Region" filter and choose "South America".
Locate the "Fieldwork year" filter and select the checkbox for "2024".
Use the sort dropdown above the results to select "Next milestone – Soonest first".
In the filtered results list, click on the first project card.
On the project details page, click the "Subscribe to updates" or "Follow project" button in the sidebar.
In the subscription form that appears, click into the email field and type "coastal.observer@example.org".
If there is a checkbox for email updates, ensure it is selected by clicking it once.
Click the "Submit" or "Subscribe" button on the subscription form.

tasks.name: Create an "Urban Air Quality" reading list with 5 PM2.5 articles (2019–2023)
tasks.description: Search for peer‑reviewed publications about urban air quality focusing on PM2.5, filter to items published between 2019 and 2023, and add the first 5 results to a new reading list titled "Urban Air Quality".
steps: From the homepage, click the "Publications" link in the main navigation.
In the Publications search box, type "PM2.5 urban air quality" and press Enter to search.
In the sidebar, open the "Publication type" filter and select the checkbox for "Peer‑reviewed article".
Adjust the "Publication year" range so the minimum year is set to 2019 and the maximum year is set to 2023.
Open the sort dropdown above the list of publications and choose "Citations – High to Low".
In the results list, click the "Add to reading list" button on each of the first five publication entries.
When a dialog asks where to save the first publication, choose "Create new list", type "Urban Air Quality" as the list name, and confirm.
Open the user toolbar or profile menu in the header and click on the "Reading lists" option.
On the Reading Lists page, click the "Urban Air Quality" list name to open the list.

tasks.name: Register for 2 climate adaptation webinars in the first half of 2025 and add them to My Schedule
tasks.description: Filter events to show climate adaptation webinars happening between 1 Jan 2025 and 30 Jun 2025, register for the first two webinars using the name "Alex Rivera" and email "alex.rivera@example.org", and add both events to the "My Schedule" section.
steps: Navigate to the homepage and click the "Events" option in the main navigation.
On the Events page, open the "Event type" filter and select the "Webinar" checkbox.
Open the "Topic" filter and select "Climate adaptation".
Use the date range controls to set the start date to "01/01/2025" and the end date to "06/30/2025".
Open the sort dropdown above the events list and choose "Date – Soonest first".
In the filtered list, click the "Register" button on the first event.
On the registration form, enter "Alex Rivera" in the name field and "alex.rivera@example.org" in the email field, then click the "Complete registration" button.
On the registration confirmation or event detail page, click the "Add to My Schedule" button for that event.
Use the navigation or breadcrumb link to return to the main Events list.

Click the "Register" button for the second event in the filtered list, fill in the name as "Alex Rivera" and email as "alex.rivera@example.org" again, submit the registration form, and then click "Add to My Schedule" for that second event.

tasks.name: Queue 2 open-license deforestation datasets from Southeast Asia for download
tasks.description: Find deforestation datasets for Southeast Asia that are available under CC-BY or CC0 licenses, restricted to CSV format, and add the first two results to the download queue before preparing a ZIP download.
steps: From the homepage, click the "Datasets" item in the main navigation.
In the dataset search bar, type "deforestation" and press Enter.
In the filters sidebar, open the "Region" filter and select "Southeast Asia".
Open the "License" filter and select the checkboxes for both "CC-BY" and "CC0".
Open the "File format" filter and select the checkbox for "CSV".
Use the sort dropdown to choose "Last updated – Newest first".
In the filtered results, click the "Add to download queue" button on the first dataset entry.
Click the "Add to download queue" button on the second dataset entry.
Click the "Download queue" icon or link in the site header to open the queue page and then click the "Download as ZIP" button to prepare the combined download.

tasks.name: Contact a medium‑risk water quality community engagement project to join its volunteer mailing list
tasks.description: Filter water quality projects to those with a community engagement component and medium risk level, open the second project, and send a message via its contact form requesting to be added to the volunteer mailing list, including interest in activities between June and December.
steps: From the homepage, click on the "Projects" item in the main navigation.
On the Projects page, open the "Topic" filter and select "Water quality".
Open the "Components" or "Tags" filter and select the checkbox labeled "Community engagement".
Open the "Risk level" filter and select the "Medium" option.
Use the sort dropdown to choose "Start date – Newest first".
In the filtered results list, click on the second project card to open its detail page.
On the project detail page, click the "Contact project team" button.
In the contact form, type "Jordan Lee" into the name field and "jordan.lee@example.net" into the email field.

In the message text area, type: "I am interested in joining the volunteer mailing list for this project, especially for activities planned between June and December. Please add me to any community outreach announcements."
Click the "Send message" button at the bottom of the contact form.

tasks.name: Save PI contact for an ongoing microplastics ocean project under $500,000 to a "Collaboration ideas" note
tasks.description: Find an ongoing ocean microplastics project with total funding not exceeding $500,000 and a team size of at most 10 researchers, then save its principal investigator contact to a new note titled "Collaboration ideas".
steps: Navigate to the homepage and click the "Projects" option in the main navigation.
In the Projects search bar, type "microplastics ocean" and press Enter.
Open the "Status" filter and select the "Ongoing" checkbox.
Find the "Budget" or "Total funding" filter and set the maximum amount to "500000".
Open the "Team size" filter and select the range option labeled "1–10 researchers".
Use the sort dropdown to select "Start date – Newest first".
In the filtered results, click on the first project card to open its details page.
On the project detail page, locate the sidebar or header section and click the "Save PI contact" or similar button.
In the dialog that appears, choose the option to "Create new note" and type "Collaboration ideas" as the note title.
In the note body field, type "Follow up with this PI about potential collaboration on microplastics research methods." and click the "Save note" button.

tasks.name: Add the longer‑duration European renewable energy project to a briefing with 3 sections
tasks.description: Filter to completed renewable energy projects in Europe, select the first two projects for comparison, choose the one with the longer project duration (or the first if they are equal), and create a briefing that includes its objectives, methods, and key outcomes.
steps: From the homepage, click the "Projects" link in the main navigation.
Open the "Topic" filter and select "Renewable energy".
Open the "Region" filter and choose "Europe".
Open the "Status" filter and select the "Completed" option.
Use the sort dropdown to select "End date – Newest first".
On the results list, click the "Add to compare" checkbox on the first project card and then on the second project card.
Click the "Compare (2)" button that appears above the project list.

On the comparison page, compare the displayed project durations in months and click the "Open details" or project title link for the project with the longer duration; if both durations are the same, click the first project's link instead.
On the selected project detail page, click the "Add to briefing" button.
In the briefing builder dialog, select checkboxes for "Objectives", "Methods", and "Key outcomes", then click the "Create briefing" button.

entities.name: Project
entities.description: Environmental research project with metadata used for search, filters, comparison, subscriptions, contact, notes, and briefings.

fields.name: components_tags
fields.description: Free-form tags such as 'Community engagement'

fields.name: has_community_engagement
fields.description: Helper flag to support the 'Community engagement' filter

fields.name: start_year
fields.description: For start year range filters

fields.name: end_year
fields.description: For end year and duration calculations

fields.name: fieldwork_years
fields.description: Array of years (numbers) when fieldwork is scheduled or occurred

fields.name: total_funding
fields.description: Total funding amount used for funding filters and sorting

fields.name: team_size
fields.description: Number of researchers on the team

fields.name: next_milestone_date
fields.description: Used for 'Next milestone – Soonest first' sorting

fields.name: duration_months
fields.description: Precomputed duration used on comparison page

entities.name: ProjectList
entities.description: Named list of projects (e.g., comparison or watch lists such as 'Africa Freshwater 2018-22').

entities.name: ProjectListItem
entities.description: Association between a project and a project list.

entities.name: ProjectUpdateSubscription
entities.description: Email subscription to updates for a project (e.g., 'Subscribe to updates' flow).

entities.name: ContactMessage
entities.description: Message sent to a project team via the 'Contact project team' form.

entities.name: Note
entities.description: Personal note, optionally linked to a project or PI contact (e.g., 'Collaboration ideas').

fields.name: related_project_id
fields.description: Project this note relates to, if any

fields.name: related_pi_name
fields.description: PI name captured when saving PI contact

fields.name: related_pi_email
fields.description: PI email captured when saving PI contact

entities.name: Briefing
entities.description: Generated project briefing including selected sections such as Objectives, Methods, and Key outcomes.

fields.name: included_sections
fields.description: Array of section identifiers included in the briefing (enum values).

fields.name: sections_enum
fields.description: Enum definition for items in included_sections.

entities.name: CompareSession
entities.description: Ephemeral set of projects selected for side-by-side comparison on the Project Comparison page.

fields.name: project_ids
fields.description: Array of project IDs included in this comparison session

entities.name: Publication
entities.description: Research publication metadata used for search, filters, and reading lists.

fields.name: citation_count
fields.description: Used for 'Citations – High to Low' sorting

fields.name: authors
fields.description: Array of author names

entities.name: ReadingList
entities.description: Named list of publications (e.g., 'Urban Air Quality').

entities.name: ReadingListItem
entities.description: Association between a publication and a reading list.

entities.name: Event
entities.description: Event or webinar related to environmental research (used for search, filters, registration, and schedules).

fields.name: speakers
fields.description: Array of speaker names

entities.name: EventRegistration
entities.description: Registration record for an event, including schedule flag for 'My Schedule'.

fields.name: added_to_schedule
fields.description: True if 'Add to My Schedule' was clicked

entities.name: Dataset
entities.description: Dataset metadata used for search, filters, and download queue.

fields.name: file_formats
fields.description: Array of file format enum values (e.g., ['csv']).

fields.name: file_format_enum
fields.description: Enum definition for items in file_formats.

fields.name: last_updated
fields.description: Used for 'Last updated – Newest first' sorting

entities.name: DownloadQueue
entities.description: Single download queue for datasets, analogous to a shopping cart.

entities.name: DownloadQueueItem
entities.description: Association between a dataset and the download queue.
